# TimeDepFrail: Time-Dependent Shared Frailty Cox Models in R

TimeDepFrail is the ultimate R package for fitting and analyzing
Time-Dependent Shared Frailty Cox Models. These models extend the
traditional Shared (Gamma) Frailty Cox Models by incorporating a
time-dependent frailty component, making it a robust tool for studying
how unexplained heterogeneity in data evolves over time.

This package implements the methods discussed in “Centre-Effect on
Survival After Bone Marrow Transplantation: Application of
Time-Dependent Frailty Models” by C.M. Wintrebert et al. (2004).

## Installation

You can install the development version of the package from `GitHub`:

    devtools::install_github("alessandragni/TimeDepFrail")

## Dataset `data_dropout`

The `data_dropout` dataset is used to exemplify the package. It tracks
the academic progress of students enrolled in 2012 over three academic
years (six semesters). This dataset aims to explore the factors leading
to student dropout.

The dataset is composed of four variables:

- `Gender`: Categorical covariate indicating gender (Male or Female).
- `CFUP`: Numeric covariate representing the standardized number of
  credits or CFUs (Credito Formativo Universitario) passed by the
  student in the first semester.
- `time_to_event`: The time (in semesters) when a student decides to
  drop out. A value greater than 6.0 means the student did not drop out
  during the follow-up period.
- `group`: Categorical variable representing the student’s course of
  study, with 16 levels from CosA to CosP.

Students are followed for a maximum of 6 semesters (3 academic years),
from the end of first semester until they drop out or the follow-up
ends.

## Model execution

To fit a Time-Dependent Shared Frailty model, the following elements are
required:

- data as `data.frame`, e.g. `data_dropout`
- `time_axis` vector: The time intervals for which the model is applied.
  For example, in the `data_dropout` dataset, no events occur in the
  first semester, so the `time_axis` starts at the end of the first
  semester (t = 1) and ends at the end of the third year (t = 6).
- `categories_range_min` and `categories_range_max` vectors: Provide
  minimum (`categories_range_min`) and maximum (`categories_range_max`)
  bounds for each parameter category to constrain the optimization.
- `formula` object: Specify the relationship between time-to-event,
  covariates, and group. For the clustering variable (`group`), it must
  be provided as `cluster(group)` in the formula.

Once these elements are prepared, you can call the desired model using
the
[`AdPaikModel()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/AdPaikModel.md)
function.

For full examples, refer to the `Examples/ReplicationCode.R` script.

Additionally, for guidance on selecting model parameters such as
`time_axis`, `categories_range_min` and `categories_range_max`, we
recommend base these choices on insights gained after fitting a
Time-Unvarying Shared Frailty model. You can find a relevant example in
the `Examples/ReplicationCode.R`, in the Appendix D Section.

## Analyzing results

Several built-in methods are available to analyze the results of the
fitted model:

- Model Summaries: [`summary()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/summary.html),
  [`plot()`](https://rdrr.io/r/graphics/plot.default.html),
  [`print()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/print.html),
  [`coef()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/coef.html),
  [`confint()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/confint.html),
  [`coefseAdPaik()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/coefseAdPaik.md),
  [`extractAIC()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/extractAIC.html),
  [`logLik()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/logLik.html)
- Baseline Hazard Step-Function:
  [`bas_hazard()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/bas_hazard.md)
  and
  [`plot_bas_hazard()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_bas_hazard.md)
- Frailty Standard Deviation/Variance:
  [`frailty_sd()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/frailty_sd.md)
  and
  [`plot_frailty_sd()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_frailty_sd.md)
- Posterior Frailty Estimates:
  [`post_frailty_est()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/post_frailty_est.md),
  [`plot_post_frailty_est()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_post_frailty_est.md),
  [`post_frailty_var()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/post_frailty_var.md),
  [`plot_post_frailty_var()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_post_frailty_var.md),
  [`post_frailty_confint()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/post_frailty_confint.md)
- Conditional Survival Function:
  [`survivalAdPaik()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/survivalAdPaik.md)
  and
  [`plot_survivalAdPaik()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_survivalAdPaik.md)

These methods provide insightful visualizations and summaries to help
you interpret your model results effectively.

Furthermore, also a support function suitable for the choice of the
range of parameters and analysis of the 1D log-likelihood is available,
[`AdPaik_1D()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/AdPaik_1D.md).

## To be aware of

- The `AdPaikModel` model is optimized for fast computation although
  estimating certain coefficients (e.g., `Male` versus `Female`) may
  vary slightly in computational time. Note that changing the reference
  category (e.g., using `Male` as the baseline) alters the coefficient
  estimates but not the overall log-likelihood or model fit. Users
  should choose reference categories based on interpretability rather
  than performance.

## Authors and maintainers of the code

Alessandra Ragni (<alessandra.ragni@polimi.it>), Giulia Romani
(<giulia.romani@mail.polimi.it>), Chiara Masci
(<chiara.masci@polimi.it>).

# Package index

## All functions

- [`AdPaikModel()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/AdPaikModel.md)
  : Adapted Paik et Al.'s Model: Time-Dependent Shared Frailty Cox Model

- [`AdPaik_1D()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/AdPaik_1D.md)
  : One-Dimensional Analysis of log-Likelihood Function

- [`bas_hazard()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/bas_hazard.md)
  : Baseline Hazard Step-Function

- [`coef(`*`<AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/coef.AdPaik.md)
  : Extract the Coefficients for the 'Adapted Paik et Al.' Model

- [`coefseAdPaik()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/coefseAdPaik.md)
  : Extracts the Standard Errors of the Coefficients for the 'Adapted
  Paik et Al.' Model

- [`confint(`*`<AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/confint.AdPaik.md)
  : Extracts the Confidence Intervals for the Coefficients for the
  'Adapted Paik et Al.' Model

- [`data_dropout`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/data_dropout.md)
  : Dropout Dataset

- [`extractAIC(`*`<AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/extractAIC.AdPaik.md)
  :

  Extract AIC for `AdPaik` Objects

- [`frailty_sd()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/frailty_sd.md)
  : Frailty Standard Deviation and Variance for the 'Adapted Paik et
  Al.'s Model'

- [`logLik(`*`<AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/logLik.AdPaik.md)
  :

  Extract Log-Likelihood for `AdPaik` Objects

- [`nobs(`*`<AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/nobs.AdPaik.md)
  :

  Extract Number of Observations for `AdPaik`

- [`plot(`*`<AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot.AdPaik.md)
  : Plots Related to the the 'Adapted Paik et Al.' Model

- [`plot_bas_hazard()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_bas_hazard.md)
  : Plot the Baseline Hazard Step-Function

- [`plot_frailty_sd()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_frailty_sd.md)
  : Plot for the Frailty Standard Deviation or Variance

- [`plot_post_frailty_est()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_post_frailty_est.md)
  : Plot the Posterior Frailty Estimates

- [`plot_post_frailty_var()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_post_frailty_var.md)
  : Plot the Posterior Frailty Variances

- [`plot_survivalAdPaik()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/plot_survivalAdPaik.md)
  : Plot of Conditional Survival Function

- [`post_frailty_confint()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/post_frailty_confint.md)
  : Posterior Frailty Confidence Intervals

- [`post_frailty_est()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/post_frailty_est.md)
  : Posterior Frailty Estimates

- [`post_frailty_var()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/post_frailty_var.md)
  : Posterior Frailty Variances

- [`print(`*`<AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/print.AdPaik.md)
  : Print method for AdPaik objects

- [`print(`*`<summary.AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/print.summary.AdPaik.md)
  : Print Method for summary.AdPaik Objects

- [`summary(`*`<AdPaik>`*`)`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/summary.AdPaik.md)
  : Summary Method for AdPaik Objects

- [`survivalAdPaik()`](https://alessandragni.github.io/TimeDepFrail/reference/survivalAdPaik.md)
  : Compute the Conditional Survival Function
